# TinyCloud Docs

TinyCloud docs are organized around reader intent and Diataxis. Start at `/`
to choose a path, then move into concepts or reference when you need the
system model or exact action names.

Full documentation corpus: https://docs.tinycloud.xyz/llms-full.txt

## Agent access quickstart

TinyCloud MCP is the separate `@tinycloud/mcp` package, installed alongside
`@tinycloud/cli`. It is a local stdio server, not a hosted HTTP endpoint and
not a subcommand embedded in `tc`. Both packages require Node.js 20 or newer.

The current MCP exploration surface is read-only. It can discover account
spaces and applications, list KV keys, read selected KV values, and read a
delegated secret. It does not currently expose generic KV write, delete, or
file-management tools. Treat file-like values according to the owning app's
manifest and schema.

Install the stable packages and create a delegated agent profile:

```sh
npm install --global @tinycloud/cli @tinycloud/mcp
tc profile create agent --posture delegate-session --operator agent
```

A fresh delegate profile must bootstrap once. The delegate emits one exact
account-registry request:

```sh
tc --profile agent --quiet --json auth request \
  --cap tinycloud.kv:account:spaces/:get,list \
  --emit request.json
```

An authenticated owner reviews and grants that exact artifact, then the
delegate imports the returned delegation:

```sh
tc --profile owner --quiet --json auth grant request.json --yes > delegation.json
tc --profile agent --quiet --json auth import delegation.json
```

Configure the MCP client to start the server with that explicit profile:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "tinycloud": {
      "command": "tinycloud-mcp",
      "args": ["--profile", "agent"]
    }
  }
}
```

Start exploration with these exact tools:

- `tinycloud_account_spaces_list` - list the canonical account space registry
- `tinycloud_account_applications_list` - list registered apps and manifests
- `tinycloud_kv_list` - list keys in one selected space or prefix
- `tinycloud_kv_get` - read one selected KV key

When a tool returns `authority_required`, preserve its returned
`tinycloud.auth.request` exactly. The owner grants that artifact without
widening it, the delegate imports the full delegation with
`tinycloud_auth_import`, and the agent retries the original tool.

Complete readable pages:

- MCP reference (Markdown): https://docs.tinycloud.xyz/cli/mcp.md
- Delegated exploration guide (Markdown): https://docs.tinycloud.xyz/guides/access-and-explore-my-tinycloud.md

## Entry points

- `/` - Homepage and reader-journey selector
- `https://docs.tinycloud.xyz/guides/access-and-explore-my-tinycloud` - MCP-first delegated account, app, and KV exploration
- `/start/browser-app` - Web SDK path from sign-in to first KV write
- `/start/backend-service` - Node SDK path and sign-in mode decision
- `/start/protocol` - Capability, manifest, and delegation vocabulary
- `/start/operator` - CLI, hosting, OpenKey, and troubleshooting path

## Build and understand

- `/quickstart/web-sdk`
- `/quickstart/node-sdk`
- `/guides/authentication`
- `/guides/browser-e2e-testing` - Browser E2E account bootstrap without passkey automation
- `/guides/spaces`
- `/guides/kv-storage`
- `/guides/delegations`
- `/guides/sharing`
- `/guides/data-vault`
- `/guides/write-hooks`
- `/guides/sql-schema-and-migrations`
- `/guides/tinycloud-vfs`
- `/guides/agent-readable-apps`
- `/concepts/system-model`
- `/concepts/security-and-trust`
- `/concepts/did-formats`
- `/concepts/capabilities`
- `/concepts/app-manifests`
- `/reference/glossary`
- `/reference/protocol/capabilities`

## Operate

- `/cli/overview`
- `https://docs.tinycloud.xyz/cli/mcp` - Local stdio MCP server, exploration tools, and delegated authorization
- `/cli/account` - Read-only live account space and application discovery commands
- `/cli/authentication`
- `/cli/storage`
- `/cli/sql`
- `/cli/duckdb`
- `/cli/spaces`
- `/cli/secrets`
- `/cli/delegations`
- `/cli/auth-artifacts`
- `/cli/profiles`
- `/cli/status`
- `/cli/manifest`
- `/cli/diagnostics`
- `/hosting/overview`
- `/hosting/configuration`
- `/hosting/docker`
- `/hosting/databases`
- `/hosting/storage`
- `/hosting/monitoring`
- `/openkey/index`
- `/openkey/quickstart`
- `/openkey/widget`
- `/openkey/oauth`
- `/openkey/tinycloud-integration`
- `/troubleshooting`

## Source and validation

- Preview locally with `mint dev`.
- Keep `reference/protocol/capabilities.mdx` synced with `npm run sync-capabilities`.
- Verify the generated registry with `npm run check-capabilities`.
